'''Karl Fitzpatrick''' (born 13 September 1980) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland international]] [[rugby league]] {{rlp|fb}}, and [[Chief executive officer]] of the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/wolves-announce-new-ceo/|title=WOLVES ANNOUNCE NEW CEO|date=2 November 2016|website=Warrington Wolves}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Fitzpatrick was originally a scrum half before he switched to full back. He is a product of [[Wigan St Patricks]]. Fitzpatrick began his professional career at [[Widnes Vikings]], followed by spells at [[Tonneins XIII]] ([[Rugby League in France|France]]) and [[Swinton Lions]].

Fitzpatrick signed for Salford in 2003. In 2004, Fitzpatrick represented [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[Rugby League European Cup]], and also picked up the most improved player award at [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref><ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|accessdate=2008-08-02|archive-date=9 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171009095326/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}}</ref>
